The domain description talks about "A rotation (fold) at node n consists of one action rotate and two following passes...".
The actions available are:
1. `rotate`: Rotates the heading of node `n` and starts the first pass.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Updates the next node in the chain based on the rotation.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Finishes the first pass when the end node is reached.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Updates the position of the next node in the chain based on the new heading.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Finishes the second pass and clears the `rotating` flag.

The `rotate` action takes parameters `?n`, `?r`, `?fromdir`, `?todir`.
It requires `heading ?n ?fromdir` and `NEXT-DIRECTION ?fromdir ?r ?todir`.
It sets `heading ?n ?todir`, adds `rotating`, and sets `node-first-pass-next ?n ?r ?n`.
This action initiates the rotation process.

The `rotate-first-pass` action takes `?nstart`, `?r`, `?n1`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`, `?n2dir`, `?n2setdir`.
It requires `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`, `NEXT-DIRECTION ?n2dir ?r ?n2setdir`, `node-first-pass-next ?nstart ?r ?n1`, `at ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y`, `heading ?n2 ?n2dir`.
It updates `heading ?n2` to `?n2setdir`, clears `at ?n2`, sets `free`, and moves the cursor to `?n2`.
This action propagates the rotation effect along the string.

The `rotate-first-pass-end` action takes `?nstart`, `?r`, `?n1`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`.
It requires `END-NODE ?n2`, `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`, `node-first-pass-next ?nstart ?r ?n1`, `at ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y`.
It clears `at ?n2`, sets `free`, clears `node-first-pass-next`, and sets `node-second-pass-next ?nstart`.

The `rotate-second-pass` action takes `?n1`, `?n1x`, `?n1y`, `?n1dir`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`.
It requires `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`, `node-second-pass-next ?n1`, `at ?n1 ?n1x ?n1y`, `heading ?n1 ?n1dir`, `free ?n2x ?n2y`.
It sets `at ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y`, clears `free`, and moves cursor to `?n2`.
This action places the nodes at their new coordinates.

The `rotate-second-pass-end` action takes `?n`.
It requires `END-NODE ?n`, `node-second-pass-next ?n`.
It clears `node-second-pass-next` and `rotating`.
